[2019-11-14 23:30] silentslayers: And there is seriously no way to fix it? Air just sucks ?
[2019-11-14 23:30] elyot: well, let me put it this way, I think it's "guaranteed" to give us like 128MB and we ask for way more than that, and usually it gives it to us
[2019-11-14 23:31] elyot: what we could do is just ask for like half a gig on startup and use that and just say "too bad" if it fails
[2019-11-14 23:31] elyot: but then some people wouldn't even be able to start the game who otherwise can run the game just fine
[2019-11-14 23:31] elyot: it's even more stupid in the browser because whether or not it gives you memory depends on how chrome feels
[2019-11-14 23:32] elyot: I probably won't develop another game in AIR for this reason, among many others
[2019-11-14 23:32] elyot: it's too important to have control of memory and it's just too temperamental
[2019-11-14 23:32] silentslayers: Fair enough. But I imagine there has to be some sort of nice solution but maybe it is just difficult?
[2019-11-14 23:33] elyot: the thing we could do is like, try to handle all the errors more gracefully
[2019-11-14 23:33] elyot: basically load in all the stuff that needs to load in, then if a skin fails to load or something, just don't display it but keep running the game
[2019-11-14 23:33] silentslayers: By difficult I mean tedious by the way, not saying you are uncapable
[2019-11-14 23:34] elyot: I think the reason it doesn't naively work nicely is that all the index/vertex buffers and shaders also need to be uploaded to the GPU
[2019-11-14 23:34] elyot: and if those fail to upload then like, we can't draw a frame
[2019-11-14 23:34] elyot: so we would also need to like, somehow ensure that it only crashes at certain times when we can definitely handle it, and I have no idea how to do that without ripping open the starling engine
[2019-11-14 23:35] elyot: which we've done for some other things, but it would likely be a huge nightmare
[2019-11-14 23:35] elyot: for the next game, David's just writing his own engine and managing his own memory and it's like 1000x simpler and easier to debug
[2019-11-14 23:35] elyot: soooooooo much better
[2019-11-14 23:36] silentslayers: Fair enough. Thanks for answering
[2019-11-14 23:37] elyot: have you tried running MSI afterburner or something like that to verify that the game is actually using the discrete GPU?
[2019-11-14 23:37] silentslayers: No. I have no idea which is using.
[2019-11-14 23:38] sano712 (sano): have you considered remaking prismata using a different engine for its "main release"?
[2019-11-14 23:38] silentslayers: I just got a new machine and haven’t been playing too much so I haven’t seen the bug on this machine yet
[2019-11-14 23:38] elyot: you can go into nvidia control panel, select prismata, and choose integrated vs discrete gpu
[2019-11-14 23:39] elyot: @sano Hahahah changing engines would take like... months and months probably
[2019-11-14 23:39] sano712 (sano): yeah I'm sure it would
[2019-11-14 23:39] elyot: For Prismata 2, if it happens, it will be a new engine, but not Prismata 1.0
[2019-11-14 23:39] sano712 (sano): I just think that prismata is an example of a game that, if released by say Valve with a decent budget, it would be a huge success
[2019-11-14 23:40] sano712 (sano): the game just suffers from the small advertisement and raw-looking UI and design
[2019-11-14 23:40] sano712 (sano): it's not very friendly to new players i think
[2019-11-14 23:40] sano712 (sano): and the fact that there are not many new players makes it even less friendly, as they jump into matchmaking and get destroyed

--- Thread (7 messages, 2019-11-14) ---
[2019-11-14 23:44] elyot: I've learned a lot about designing to protect new players, I think it's sorta too late to change Prismata now, but our initial focus was just making a hardcore competitive experience that we ourselves would want
[2019-11-14 23:46] elyot: but there is sorta a massive unfair advantage that, e.g. blizzard games have in that there are soooo many players queuing
[2019-11-14 23:46] elyot: so you can have low queue times and good matches without much tradeoff
[2019-11-14 23:47] elyot: I think for smaller/indie games you need to design in advance to avoid having to make that tradeoff
[2019-11-14 23:47] elyot: like, if you compare to games where you fight against recordings of other players, or AIs, they don't have that problem
[2019-11-14 23:49] elyot: PvE doesn't have that problem either but I think there are other types of content that are a lot better than puzzles
[2019-11-14 23:49] elyot: (I like puzzles though)

[2019-12-05 22:03] elyot: I think it will come down to one big push with transitioning from early access to full release, putting out the last 2 campaign chapters, and launching on some other platforms

[2019-12-05 22:05] elyot: we did a lot of shit with ads and we sponsored some esports
[2019-12-05 22:07] elyot: like it basically comes down to cost of paid user acquisition, how much of an organic multiplier it generates (hardly any unfortunately), and how well we monetize (barely break even it seems)

[2019-12-05 22:07] elyot: but I think after you transition from early access to full release, it gets better

[2019-12-05 22:19] elyot: full release I don't know, honestly I'm the only one working on the game right now and even then it's not full time so it's hard to make a lot of progress quickly
[2019-12-05 22:20] elyot: and all the work I do on Prismata is unpaid, even not counting that, we've all lost a lot of money on it
[2019-12-05 22:22] elyot: I mean we expected that to be the median result when we started, like most startups fail even if the idea is really good cuz sometimes you just don't end up having a huge market
[2019-12-05 22:22] elyot: and you take the risk because sometimes you might strike it rich and discover something that a lot of people never knew they wanted
[2019-12-05 22:22] elyot: and at the very least, you get to work on something really new and exciting
[2019-12-05 22:23] elyot: my personal financial situation is fine so there's no issue, it's just hard to convince other people to help when Lunarch doesn't have much cash and the game doesn't show a big sign of achieving a massive success
[2019-12-05 22:23] zezetel: the real treasure is the friends you made on the way :kappa:
[2019-12-05 22:24] elyot: yeah and also like, you learn so much when you take on a project like this, and a lot of the stuff you learn will help make the next one better
[2019-12-05 22:24] elyot: I was literally talking to somebody last week who said we should just raise a bunch of money and redo the game with 3d graphics and make it a bit more casual-friendly
[2019-12-05 22:24] elyot: because the underlying concept is so much fun
[2019-12-05 22:24] zezetel: you can make prismata in baba is you.  its turing complete
[2019-12-05 22:25] elyot: but I think the team is too exhausted to like, start all over again, we need a couple of other projects first before we even think about prismata 2 lol
[2019-12-05 22:25] elyot: that's the other problem, new projects are way too sexy and exciting so people feel less motivated to work on Prismata

[2019-12-05 22:26] elyot: I may spend 2020 trying to get funding for a different kind of project entirely, might build a prototype over the holidays

[2019-12-19 06:47] ruinedshadows: Sad to see so many players on hiatus or leaving, I have kind of been gone a bit recently too. I'm anticipating having some more time in the new year and, as always, I'm ready and willing to dedicate my time to organizing things for the community if there is interest. I was thinking about some kind of competitive league. At this rate though, maybe we'll just do an annual summer tourney or something.
[2019-12-19 06:49] ruinedshadows: As far as the current conversation going on in #ask_a_dev ,  I'm glad there is still some thought going into how to make the game feasible long term and at least have the opportunity for future growth and I'm happy to hear there is progress on Chapter 4, even though it has taken a lot longer than originally anticipated.
[2019-12-19 06:50] ruinedshadows: Would love it if a reworked business model made it more worthwhile for the game to advertise to players. I'd think that something like $25 for the base game, discounted somtimes, plus the same tiered options that exist now could be reasonable. I also think a battle pass type system could work. Personally, I paid to join Prismata as it wasn't F2P yet and am still happy with that purchase as well as my other upgrades. I wouldn't be mad if the game went back to that business model, plus maybe sometimes free weekends or free keys could be given out, especially to the occasional interested player that digs into the game or discord and is really interested in playing, as it helps to have more dedicated players, even F2P ones.
[2019-12-19 06:50] lycomedes6482 (Lycomedes): i think ranked and leagues are the main appeal, not the campaign
[2019-12-19 06:51] lycomedes6482 (Lycomedes): and the current lack of players makes even more quit, so we're approaching 0 xd
[2019-12-19 06:53] ruinedshadows: Yeah it's definitely a snowball effect (in both directions)
[2019-12-19 06:55] lycomedes6482 (Lycomedes): as for a $25 price, if no one is picking up the game now that it's free, i don't think they will if it costs money
[2019-12-19 06:56] ruinedshadows: I disagree, it's weird how it works but if people feel like they've invested something they will take it more seriously
[2019-12-19 06:56] ruinedshadows: Plus, I've felt like some of the advertising combined with the initial menu UI turns some players off who think it's just a freemium mobile game and they're looking for a strategy game
[2019-12-19 06:57] lycomedes6482 (Lycomedes): the advertising wasn't very good y
[2019-12-19 06:57] lycomedes6482 (Lycomedes): the aoe stream was pretty good
[2019-12-19 06:57] lycomedes6482 (Lycomedes): but it didn't bring in any players
[2019-12-19 06:57] ruinedshadows: which is better than 0 from stuff like the twitch bounties
[2019-12-19 06:59] ruinedshadows: Plus, I think there's a good chance many of the players who look at it if free would also have paid 10 or 20 dollars (especially just picking up a good looking game in a sale or something) and that wouldn't initially net more players perhaps (if it just sits in steam libraries) but it would generate some revenue which could be used to advertise to get more players and to keep the dev work going
[2019-12-19 07:00] ruinedshadows: There are some glaring things that I think need to be fixed but Elyot can't do everything himself and it must be at least a little demoralizing at this point
[2019-12-19 07:00] ruinedshadows: feeling like he is just pouring hours of time into something that both doesn't generate money or players or allow for the type of growth potential to get the game to critical mass must not be great
